answers.answer: B) The system must handle image resizing, storage limits, and content moderation for inappropriate images. These are critical implicit requirements that if not addressed can cause security issues, storage problems, and legal/compliance risks. Option A (PNG format) is too specific - multiple formats should be supported. Option C (filters) is nice-to-have, not required. Option D (blue button) is UI preference, not functional requirement.

IMPLICIT REQUIREMENTS BY CATEGORY:

SECURITY: CSRF protection, input sanitization (XSS prevention), rate limiting, CAPTCHA/bot protection, email validation.

VALIDATION: Name (required, max length), Email (required, valid format), Message (required, min/max length).

BACKEND: Email delivery service, email templates, database/storage, error handling/logging, retry logic.

COMPLIANCE: Privacy policy link (GDPR/CCPA), consent checkbox, data retention policy.

UX: Success/error messages, validation feedback, loading state, mobile responsiveness, accessibility (WCAG).

OPERATIONAL: Where submissions go (email/CRM/dashboard), who receives notifications, spam filtering, auto-reply.

TIMELINE: 'End of day' unrealistic - MVP takes 4-8 hours, production-ready takes 1-2 days.

CLARIFYING QUESTIONS: Where should submissions be stored? Need email notifications? Confirmation email to user? Any branding requirements? Privacy policy in place?
